Title: CYP82G1 Enzyme Functions as Chromone Hydroxylase in the Biosynthesis of 6-Hydroxy-2-(2-Phenylethyl)chromone in Aquilaria sinensis

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Aquilaria sinensis; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Aquilaria sinensis; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: wild A. sinensis; NA; WAT11; Rosetta (DE3)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key quality marker and bioactive constituent of agarwood, contributing to fragrance, medicinal activity, and water solubility-related properties of agarwood PECs
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem resinous agarwood tissues / wounded stem regions
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Hydroxylation modification branch of PEC diversification; C6 hydroxylation step
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytochrome P450 monooxygenase; CYP82 family; CYP71 clan
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates 2-(2-phenylethyl)chromone at the C6 position to form 6-hydroxy-2-(2-phenylethyl)chromone
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream modification step in PEC diversification
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Critical hydroxylation step for highly oxygenated PEC formation; rate-limiting not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in stems after drilling/wounding; expression higher in wild A. sinensis than in Qi-Nan A. sinensis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transient expression in N. benthamiana leaves and A. sinensis stems produced target hydroxylated PEC; overexpression in A. sinensis stems increased transcript level 7.12-fold
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drilling/wounding treatment; exogenous substrate feeding with 2-(2-phenylethyl)chromone in heterologous assays
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pBI121 CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for transient plant overexpression; galactose-inducible pESC-HIS in yeast; IPTG-inducible pCDFDuet-1 in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Arabidopsis thaliana CPR1; Arabidopsis thaliana ATR1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of 6-hydroxy-2-(2-phenylethyl)chromone by catalyzing C6 hydroxylation of PEC; transient expression caused ~8.41-fold higher product in N. benthamiana and 2.57-fold higher product in A. sinensis stems
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transcriptome/WGCNA and metabolite correlation identified AsCYP82G1 as a strong candidate linked to hydroxylated PEC accumulation. 2. Yeast microsome and E. coli coexpression assays demonstrated conversion of 2-(2-phenylethyl)chromone into 6-hydroxy-2-(2-phenylethyl)chromone. 3. Transient expression in N. benthamiana and A. sinensis validated in vivo C6-hydroxylase function. 4. Docking and QM analysis suggested Met222 and Arg223 are important catalytic residues and supported a feasible epoxidation/ring-opening mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: WGCNA-guided candidate selection; qRT-PCR; yeast microsome enzyme assay; E. coli coexpression with reductase; LC-MS/MS metabolite identification; Agrobacterium transient expression in N. benthamiana; transient overexpression in A. sinensis stems; molecular docking; quantum mechanical calculation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics (42 RNA-seq libraries, Illumina NovaSeq 6000, de novo assembly); targeted LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling of PECs; accession number not reported, data stated as Data S1 and S2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Industrial deployment will require completion of the full PEC pathway and may depend on wound-induced context; broader metabolic effects and growth penalties were not tested
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for breeding high-quality agarwood varieties and for synthetic biology production of hydroxylated PECs. Can be stacked with downstream O-methyltransferases such as AsOMT11/AsOMT16 to produce methoxylated PECs. Catalytic residues Met222/Arg223 provide targets for enzyme engineering toward enhanced activity or altered substrate scope.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Initially identified as Unigene0047038/AsCYP217. The paper compares it with AsCYP4 (Unigene0000389), but only AsCYP82G1 received full functional validation. The study reports that CYP82 family function is expanded here from known terpene-related activities to chromone hydroxylation.
